Paste Images Straight to Imgur from VSCode

I switched to VSCode for writing posts, but uploading images through the Imgur website and copying the URL back into the Markdown file every single time was painful. I found an extension that solves this — it looks like this:
So here's how to install and use it.
This post covers Imgur, but after 2025-05-16 Imgur stopped letting Taiwan IPs upload images. If you're in Taiwan, you might want to switch to a different image hosting service.
, register an account and log in. Then head to
Authorization type: OAuth 2 authorization without a callback URL
After submitting, you'll get two things — make sure to copy and save them.
If you lose them, you can look them up here, but Client secret will need to be regenerated.
Note: don't close the page yet — we'll come back to it.
Now install the extension. Search for
(Windows) — or go to Settings and find
language-json shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
Now find any image, right-click and copy it.
In your Markdown file in VSCode, hit
. It'll pause and pop this dialog:
Switch over to your browser — Imgur will say vscode-imgur wants to upload images on your behalf. Hit Allow.
Imgur will show you a PIN. Copy it.
Paste the PIN back in VSCode:
Done — you can now upload images directly.
If you want uploaded images sorted into an album, head to Imgur and create one.
Fill in the fields. The bottom option is up to you — I use public.
After creating, I'd suggest uploading one image to it first. Then click your gallery profile at the top.
If you uploaded an image already, you'll see it here. Click into it.
Grab the album code from the URL — in my case it's
That's it — try pasting images directly into VSCode now.

html pre.shiki code .sWayO, html code.shiki .sWayO{--shiki-light:#032F62;--shiki-default:#032F62;--shiki-dark:#ADDCFF}html pre.shiki code .s8XSl, html code.shiki .s8XSl{--shiki-light:#24292E;--shiki-default:#24292E;--shiki-dark:#F0F3F6}html pre.shiki code .stw71, html code.shiki .stw71{--shiki-light:#005CC5;--shiki-default:#005CC5;--shiki-dark:#91CBFF}html .light .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-light);background: var(--shiki-light-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-light-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-light-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-light-text-decoration);}html.light .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-light);background: var(--shiki-light-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-light-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-light-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-light-text-decoration);}html .default .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}html.dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}html pre.shiki code .sGBqm, html code.shiki .sGBqm{--shiki-light:#6A737D;--shiki-default:#6A737D;--shiki-dark:#BDC4CC}
